Each phase must be completed before the next can begin. We need to break down each phase into concrete subtasks and assign them to agents based on their expertise.

The habitat construction phase can be broken down into the following subtasks: site selection, design and planning, resource acquisition, and construction execution. Each of these subtasks will require collaboration and expertise from various agents to ensure successful completion. Assigning these subtasks effectively is critical for the overall success of the mission.

The life support systems phase can be broken down into the following subtasks: oxygen generation, water recycling, waste management, and environmental control. Each subtask will depend on the completion of habitat construction to ensure all systems are effectively integrated. Proper assignment of these tasks is vital for establishing a sustainable living environment on Mars.
